edit_summary: Round-2 Reviewer B's single new Major (EDGE-CASE-COVERAGE: AC-006's asymmetric fetch-failure cases were outside TEST-006's fixture scope) is resolved by rewriting the TEST-006 row in acceptance-tests.md from a single both-vendors-fail fixture into three explicit scenarios — both vendors fail, Anthropic fails while OpenAI succeeds, and OpenAI fails while Anthropic succeeds — each asserting exit 0, a recorded fetch-unavailable comment call, and zero issue-create calls, with the two asymmetric scenarios additionally asserting that the fail-soft path is taken without computing a divergence from the surviving vendor's partial data alone (no partial-data issue filing); the existing stubbed-gh/no-live-network CI-resilience wording is preserved unchanged. Matching consistency updates were made in design.md (test-technique step 3 expanded to the same three scenarios with the by-construction proof that main exits fail-soft on the first fetch failure before compute_divergence runs; Test Strategy item 2 notes TEST-006 now covers all three shapes AC-006's either wording implies) and in security-spec.md's Security Tests TEST-006 row. requirements.md's AC-006 wording was already correct (either) and is unchanged, no AC or TEST IDs were added, removed, or renumbered, and the AC/TEST mapping remains 1:1 at 21 pairs (grep-verified); infra-spec.md already stated a fetch failure for EITHER vendor and needed no change.
